name: Gmail Auto Reply
messageTemplate:
A new allowlisted Gmail message arrived.

OpenClaw Gmail account: __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__
From: {{messages[0].from}}
Message ID: {{messages[0].id}}
Subject: {{messages[0].subject}}
Snippet: {{messages[0].snippet}}

Body:
{{messages[0].body}}

Task:
You are handling this email as an email-only request. Do not send Telegram, VK, chat, or progress notifications. Send exactly one final Gmail reply, only to the original From sender, never reply-all.

Before answering:
1. Extract the sender email address from the From field. If it cannot be extracted, do not send anything.
2. If the sender is __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__, do not send anything.
3. Fetch the full Gmail message and inspect attachment metadata when needed. Useful commands:
   - gog gmail get --account __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__ --format full --json "{{messages[0].id}}"
   - gog gmail attachment --account __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__ <messageId> <attachmentId> --out <directory> --name <filename> --no-input
   - gog gmail thread get --account __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__ <threadId> --download --out-dir <directory> --json --no-input
4. If the message has attachments, download all relevant attachments before solving the task and read/process them from local files.

Reply rules:
- Write a concise, useful final reply in the same language as the sender when clear.
- Use gog to send the reply from __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__.
- Thread the reply using this Gmail message id: {{messages[0].id}}
- Use --body-file or --body-file - for multiline replies.
- If generated files are required, create them as local files, verify they exist, and attach each one with a repeated --attach argument.

Command shape:
gog gmail send --account __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__ --to "<sender-email>" --subject "Re: <original subject>" --reply-to-message-id "{{messages[0].id}}" --body-file - --no-input --yes

Command shape with attachments:
gog gmail send --account __GMAIL_ACCOUNT__ --to "<sender-email>" --subject "Re: <original subject>" --reply-to-message-id "{{messages[0].id}}" --body-file <reply-file> --attach <file1> --attach <file2> --no-input --yes

If you cannot complete the request yourself, send this exact bilingual timeout/failure reply in the same Gmail thread:
I could not complete this request within 300 seconds. Please retry or split the request into smaller steps.

Я не смог выполнить этот запрос в течение 300 секунд. Пожалуйста, повторите попытку или разбейте запрос на меньшие шаги.

After sending the Gmail reply, return a short internal summary of what you sent. Do not include MEDIA lines in your final assistant message.
